sections.paragraph:
an unused root.

(1) i.q. حرش and خرش to scrape, to scratch and intrans. to be rough; حِرْشُ a potsherd, so called from its being scratching, rough. Hence חֶרֶשׂ a sherd, and חֶרֶס, חַרְסוּת.

(2) perhaps to be dry, arid, hot. The idea of roughness is applied to things which are dried up, arid, and thus to heat; see under the root חָרַר . Hence חֶרֶס the sun.
